questions.question: A bag contains 5 red balls, 3 blue balls, and 2 green balls. You draw two balls without replacement. What is the probability that the second ball is blue given that the first ball was red? Express your answer as a fraction in lowest terms.
questions.explanation: After drawing one red ball, 9 balls remain in the bag (4 red, 3 blue, 2 green). The conditional probability is the number of blue balls divided by the total remaining: 3/9 = 1/3.

questions.question: In a two-way table, 120 students were surveyed about whether they play sports (yes/no) and whether they play music (yes/no). 45 play both, 30 play only sports, 25 play only music, and 20 play neither. If a student is randomly selected and plays sports, what is the probability they also play music?
questions.explanation: Among students who play sports: 45 play both, and 30 play only sports, totaling 75. P(music | sports) = 45/75 = 3/5.

questions.question: A test for a rare disease has a 98% true positive rate (sensitivity) and a 95% true negative rate (specificity). The disease affects 0.5% of the population. If a randomly selected person tests positive, what is the probability they actually have the disease? Round to three decimal places.
Two fair six-sided dice are rolled. What is the probability that the sum is at least 10, given that at least one die shows a 5?

A company manufactures widgets. The probability a widget passes quality control is 0.92. If 3 widgets are selected independently, what is the probability that at least 2 pass? Round to three decimal places.
questions.explanation: P(at least 2 pass) = P(exactly 2) + P(exactly 3). P(exactly 2) = C(3,2)(0.92)²(0.08) ≈ 0.202. P(exactly 3) = (0.92)³ ≈ 0.778. Sum ≈ 0.988.

questions.question: In a deck of 52 cards, you draw 2 cards without replacement. Given that the first card is a King, what is the probability the second card is also a King?
questions.explanation: After drawing one King, 51 cards remain, of which 3 are Kings. The conditional probability is 3/51 = 1/17.
